reasoning: Arih1 encodes an RBR‑type E3 ubiquitin ligase that targets several signaling intermediates for proteasomal degradation. In immune cells it has been reported to ubiquitinate components of the NF‑κB pathway, acting as a negative regulator of NF‑κB activation. CRISPRi‑mediated knockdown of Arih1 therefore relieves this inhibition, leading to heightened NF‑κB signaling. NF‑κB is a major transcriptional activator of the anti‑inflammatory cytokine Il10 in macrophages and dendritic cells. Consequently, reduced Arih1 levels are expected to increase Il10 transcription.
